title: Podman Go API gives a segmentaion fault when I try to limit a container's memory
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? **

/kind bug

**Description**

Hi, I am trying to use the Go Api in order to limit the memory a container at the moment of its creation. It looks like when I try to do it, a segmentation fault occurs. Maybe the problem is just I misunderstood how to use the Api function, but can you tell me if this is the correct way to limit a container's memory?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

s := specgen.NewSpecGenerator(image, false)
s.Image = image
memory_limit := (1024 * 1048576)  // Define the memory limit
s.ResourceLimits.Memory.Limit = &memory_limit  // Define the pointer to it
r, err := containers.CreateWithSpec(ctx, s, new(containers.CreateOptions))

**Describe the results you received:**
Indipendentely from the number I use to set memory limit, the container doesn't start and the application crashes giving a segmentation fault. If I comment the lines regarding the limit, everything works fine.

**Describe the results you expected:**
The container should start with the limit set correctly

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 4.4.0-dev
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

comments.body: perhaps you could provide the backtrace?
and a reproducer example that is complete would also speed up the answer

Here you go, this is the backtrace when I try to run the code I've written above:

panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x18 pc=0xf5a359]

goroutine 99 [running]:
github.com/matteo/s/internal/container.(*PodmanFactory).Create(0xc0001d1970, {0xc0000d20f0, 0x2b}, 0xc000328400)
        /home/matteo/Documenti/Workspaces/Tesi/s/internal/container/podman.go:55 +0x219
github.com/matteo/s/internal/container.NewContainer({0xc0000d20f0?, 0x0?}, {0xc0004b7000, 0xaac}, 0x0?)
        /home/matteo/Documenti/Workspaces/Tesi/s/internal/container/container.go:17 +0x56
github.com/matteo/s/internal/node.NewContainerWithAcquiredResources(0xc0001baba0)
        /home/matteo/Documenti/Workspaces/Tesi/s/internal/node/pool.go:195 +0x1a5
github.com/matteo/s/internal/node.NewContainer(0xc0001baba0)
        /home/matteo/Documenti/Workspaces/Tesi/s/internal/node/pool.go:176 +0x5f
github.com/matteo/s/internal/scheduling.handleColdStart(0xc0005b8360)
        /home/matteo/Documenti/Workspaces/Tesi/s/internal/scheduling/scheduler.go:325 +0x2e
github.com/matteo/s/internal/scheduling.(*DefaultLocalPolicy).OnArrival(0xc0001d1660, 0xc0005b8360)
        /home/matteo/Documenti/Workspaces/Tesi/s/internal/scheduling/policy_default.go:80 +0xa5
created by github.com/matteo/s/internal/scheduling.Run
        /home/matteo/Documenti/Workspaces/Tesi/s/internal/scheduling/scheduler.go:96 +0x49e


To reproduce the issue it is enough to run the instruction I've written in my previous message. Removing/commenting the lines 

memory_limit := (1024 * 1048576) // Define the memory limit
s.ResourceLimits.Memory.Limit = &memory_limit // Define the pointer to it

allows the container creation without a segmentation fault.
both `ResourceLimits` and `Memory` are also a pointers so you need to make sure they point to a valid struct before you can set the limit on them

Oh silly me, thank you for the help, I added the lines you suggested me and it seems to work now. For people who will read this the code is:

s := specgen.NewSpecGenerator(image, false)
s.Image = image
memory_limit := (1024 * 1048576) // Define the memory limit
s.ResourceLimits = new(specs.LinuxResources)
s.ResourceLimits.Memory = new(specs.LinuxMemory)
s.ResourceLimits.Memory.Limit = &memory_limit // Define the pointer to it
r, err := containers.CreateWithSpec(ctx, s, new(containers.CreateOptions))


Thanks :)
